examples.title: Simple Consensus Simulation
examples.description: Python simulation of basic majority voting consensus
examples.code:
import random

class Node:
    def __init__(self, node_id, is_byzantine=False):
        self.node_id = node_id
        self.is_byzantine = is_byzantine
        self.proposed_value = None
        self.received_values = []
    
    def propose(self, value):
        if self.is_byzantine:
            # Byzantine node may propose random value
            self.proposed_value = random.choice([value, 'FAKE_VALUE'])
        else:
            self.proposed_value = value
        return self.proposed_value
    
    def receive(self, value):
        self.received_values.append(value)
    
    def decide(self):
        # Simple majority voting
        from collections import Counter
        counts = Counter(self.received_values)
        return counts.most_common(1)[0][0]

def run_consensus(n_nodes, n_byzantine, proposed_value):
    # Create nodes (some Byzantine)
    nodes = []
    for i in range(n_nodes):
        is_byzantine = i < n_byzantine
        nodes.append(Node(i, is_byzantine))
    
    # Each node proposes a value
    proposals = [node.propose(proposed_value) for node in nodes]
    print(f"Proposals: {proposals}")
    
    # Broadcast to all nodes
    for node in nodes:
        for proposal in proposals:
            node.receive(proposal)
    
    # Each honest node decides
    decisions = [node.decide() for node in nodes if not node.is_byzantine]
    print(f"Honest node decisions: {decisions}")
    
    # Check consensus
    if len(set(decisions)) == 1:
        print(f"Consensus reached: {decisions[0]}")
    else:
        print("Consensus FAILED!")

# Run with 7 nodes, 2 Byzantine
run_consensus(7, 2, 'BLOCK_123')
examples.explanation: This simulation shows basic majority voting. Honest nodes propose the true value, Byzantine nodes may lie. With f Byzantine nodes, we need n >= 3f+1 total nodes for consensus.

examples.title: Consensus State Machine
examples.description: JavaScript implementation of consensus state transitions
examples.explanation: State machine showing IDLE -> PROPOSED -> COMMITTED transitions. Demonstrates threshold voting for BFT consensus requiring 2f+1 votes out of 3f+1 nodes.

examples.title: Consensus Interface in Go
examples.description: Go interface defining consensus mechanism contract
examples.explanation: This Go interface shows how production blockchain frameworks define consensus engines. Any consensus mechanism (PoW, PoS, PBFT) must implement this interface.
